summary: iOS | Morpheus | Unable to track inCall status
scenario: Morpheus devices fail to report in-call status via deckardId 0x0E35, preventing proper DFU blocking and ANC transparency mode control during active calls.
description_clean: The iOS application cannot reliably determine if a user is in a call when connected to Morpheus devices. This prevents the app from showing appropriate alerts or blocking DFU initiation and ANC transparency mode switching during an active call. The root cause is that Morpheus devices do not send or respond to deckardId 0x0E35. The fix is to also use deckardId 0x0E22, which correctly reflects call state on Morpheus devices. Acceptance criteria: all devices correctly respond to in-call status; DFU availability is enabled/blocked based on call state; ANC transparency control is enabled/disabled accordingly.
steps: Connect iOS application to a Morpheus device
Initiate an active call on the iOS device
Observe the app's ability to detect the in-call status
Attempt to initiate DFU (Device Firmware Update) while in the call
Attempt to switch ANC transparency mode while in the call
Apply the fix to also use deckardId 0x0E22 for call state detection
Re-test steps 2-5 with the fix applied
expected: All devices correctly respond to in-call status; DFU availability is blocked during an active call; ANC transparency control is disabled during an active call.
actual: The iOS application cannot reliably determine if a user is in a call when connected to Morpheus devices. This prevents the app from showing appropriate alerts or blocking DFU initiation and ANC transparency mode switching during an active call.
